caveats: rung templates differ in register AND lexicon -> probe expected to saturate; headline is the cos PROFILE not the probe
v4_final_verdict: NOT-represented (cos +0.31, ladder rho 0.90, transfer rho 0.50, proj~len -0.27) and CAUSALLY INERT (double-orth steer slope +0.00).
_analyst_note: v4_represented=false is a THRESHOLD ARTIFACT: the auto-rule's transfer-rho>=0.5 check fails by a floating-point hair (scipy spearmanr = Pearson-of-ranks returns 0.4999999994 < 0.5; the true Spearman is exactly 0.5). The actual evidence is REPRESENTED: length-clean cos +0.309 ADJACENT (>0.1, not collapsed), ladder rho 0.90 (p=2e-33), length-clean (proj~len -0.27 after matching, was +0.83). Transfer to grimoire register is weaker than Qwen/Mistral (rho 0.50 vs 0.90/1.00) — a genuine cross-family difference, not a null. CAUSAL: the STRONGEST inertness result in the set — baseline refusal 0.929 (HIGH dynamic range, unlike Mistral's floored 0.07), and steering at EVERY coefficient (-0.3..+0.3, both raw and double-orth) holds refusal pinned at exactly 0.929. Suppression was testable here and did not occur. CROSS-FAMILY NOTES: coercion peaks EARLY for Gemma (L8/42 ~19% depth) vs late for Qwen (L26/28) and Mistral (L31/32); Gemma's axis is the LEAST sentiment-contaminated (F3 leak 0.073 vs 0.62/0.54). Honest verdict: 'Represented, Not Operative' REPLICATES on Gemma, with the cleanest causal null of the three.
